title: ProtocolLab MASQUE CONNECT-UDP Contracts
purpose: Define a deterministic RFC 9298 CONNECT-UDP tunnel workload with explicit client, proxy, UDP target, datagram, validation, metric, and artifact semantics.
scope: The initial family covers one authenticated CONNECT-UDP tunnel over HTTP/3 to one package-backed UDP echo target and 32 deterministic datagram round trips. It does not cover ordinary HTTP proxying, CONNECT-IP, IP tunneling, arbitrary external targets, multiple tunnels, or browser APIs.

requirements.title: Explicit three-role topology
requirements.statement: The MASQUE CONNECT-UDP scenario MUST declare distinct client, proxy, and UDP target roles and MUST identify the exact proxy authority, URI template, target host, and target port.

requirements.title: Exact CONNECT-UDP over HTTP/3 binding
requirements.statement: A MASQUE result MUST prove an HTTP/3 Extended CONNECT request with the connect-udp protocol, HTTP Datagrams, Context ID zero, a successful response, and no protocol fallback or ordinary HTTP proxy substitution.

requirements.title: Deterministic datagram semantics
requirements.statement: Each operation MUST send exactly 32 ordered 256-byte datagrams generated by the declared index-plus-octet modulo-251 function and MUST validate the complete echoed payload-set SHA-256 with zero loss.

requirements.title: MASQUE validity gate and retained evidence
requirements.statement: A passing MASQUE observation MUST retain protocol, tunnel, target-role, datagram-count, byte-count, payload-hash, operation-outcome, stdout, stderr, and normalized result evidence for the client, proxy, and UDP target.

requirements.title: MASQUE comparison isolation
requirements.statement: MASQUE comparisons MUST match scenario version, RFC 9298 binding, URI template, target identity, datagram shape and hash, roles, lifecycle, load profile, network profile, platform, topology, validation, and repetition policy.
